full_text:
👁 **The smoking gun? This patent may be at the center of US weather warfare against Iran**

➡️ Years before the bombs started dropping, Iran accused the US and its allies of using [weather manipulation technology](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/68730) to “steal” rain clouds and create artificial droughts. The claims were widely mocked and derided.

➡️ Then the war began, Iran [knocked out a network of US military facilities](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/68511) across the region, and drought conditions seemingly [ended almost overnight](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/68647?single).

**Now, online investigators have exposed the technology that may be behind it all:**

🔴 US Patent 2022/0002159 AI – “3D Graphene Oxide Nanoparticles for Cloud Seeding.”

🔴 Created by UAE-based Khalifa University researchers Linda Zhou and Haoran Liang, the patent application document describes an “ice-nucleating particle for cloud seeding and other applications” that “can initiate ice nucleation at a temperature of -8° C.”

🔴 Translation? A tool to manipulate rain or snowfall, fog and hail at low altitudes using graphene oxide – the same compound behind almost every ‘chemtrail’ and mRNA ‘conspiracy theory’ discussion over the last decade.

🔴 ‘Fact-checkers’ assure that manipulating weather using these kinds of particles is presently impossible, citing the massive amounts of power a ground station built to purpose would require. But what if it’s enough to target lower-flying, warmer clouds, releasing a small amount of material and triggering a chain reaction?

🔴 NASA, the DoE and MIT have been tinkering with acoustic levitation technology since at least the 80s and 90s, with MIT even creating specialized test facilities specifically for cloud physics experiments (see the 1991 article “Development and Testing of the MIT Acoustic Levitation Test Facilities”).

full_text:
**🔍****Nothing to see here: Trump wants new domestic spying powers after convenient lone gunman attack**

💬“I’m willing to give up my security for the military because ultimately for me that’s the highest cause, the safety of our nation,” Trump said, not even 24 hours after the [White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/68800), pushing legislation that would give intel agencies a freer hand to spy on Americans.

👉Trump’s comments come two days after House Speaker Johnson made a new push to extend Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA) – which allows for the warrantless intercept of the communications of Americans speaking to foreigners placed on a special watchlist. 

❗️Civil rights groups call the powers a blatant violation of constitutional protections and point to their abuse against protesters, journalists, lawmakers and even Trump himself (Russiagate).

🤡Nevertheless, Trump says he’s ready to “risk” his personal “rights and privileges as a citizen” for security.

__“Those who would give up essential Liberty to purchase a little temporary Safety, deserve neither.” - Benjamin Franklin.__

full_text:
🤥 **Trump's blockade of Iran strategy hits a wall **

While Donald Trump resorted to naval blockades to pressure the governments of Venezuela and Cuba, Iran presents major differences when it comes to the US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

🔴 Unlike Cuba or Venezuela, Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz for energy shipments, which means that the longer the standoff persists, the more the global economy will be affected 

🔴 It gives Iran leverage during the fragile ceasefire, which could in turn force Trump to end the blockade of the strait 

🔴 Iran also poses a greater military threat than Cuba and Venezuela, and requires a sustained US military presence far from its shores

“Blockades are usually just one tool of a mechanism used in a conflict," says maritime historian Salvatore Mercogliano. "I don't think it's going to be enough to convince the Iranians."

__The math is brutal:__

➡️ Iran has leverage over the Strait of Hormuz

➡️ Longer standoff = higher US gas prices (election year)

➡️ US pain tolerance may be lower than Iran’s
